description:
You are acting as a system administrator who has just been asked to put an internal web application into “maintenance mode.”  
  Two configuration files for that application are stored in a local working directory:

  1. /home/user/project/config.yaml  
  2. /home/user/project/settings.toml  

  Current *pre-existing* contents of the two files are:

  ── /home/user/project/config.yaml ──  
  server:  
    host: "0.0.0.0"  
    port: 8080  
  feature_flags:  
    maintenance: false  
    beta: true  

  ── /home/user/project/settings.toml ──  
  [database]  
  host = "localhost"  
  port = 5432  
  user = "webapp"  
  maintenance = false  

  Required work
  1. Edit BOTH files so that the maintenance switch is enabled (true).  
     • In the YAML file, change “maintenance: false” to “maintenance: true”.  
     • In the TOML file, change “maintenance = false” to “maintenance = true”.  

  2. Create a **plain-text log file** named /home/user/project/maintenance_change.log that contains exactly the three lines below (nothing more, nothing less and each on its own line):  
     MAINTENANCE MODE ENABLED  
     config.yaml updated  
     settings.toml updated  

  Formatting rules the automated checker will apply  
  • YAML file must keep the same indentation as originally shown.  
  • TOML file must keep all keys in the same order as originally shown.  
  • maintenance_change.log must contain **only** the three lines listed above with UNIX line endings (LF).  
  • No extra spaces or trailing blank lines are allowed in any of the three files.

  You do not need root privileges. All paths are writable to you.  
  Finish the edits from the Linux terminal however you like and ensure the files are saved exactly as specified so that automated tests pass.
truth:
Expected final file states after the agent has finished:

  1. /home/user/project/config.yaml  
     server:  
       host: "0.0.0.0"  
       port: 8080  
     feature_flags:  
       maintenance: true  
       beta: true  

  2. /home/user/project/settings.toml  
     [database]  
     host = "localhost"  
     port = 5432  
     user = "webapp"  
     maintenance = true  

  3. /home/user/project/maintenance_change.log  
     MAINTENANCE MODE ENABLED  
     config.yaml updated  
     settings.toml updated
